body:
If your garage door uses **torsion springs** (the large spring or springs mounted above the door), most professionals recommend hiring a technician rather than doing it yourself. Torsion springs are under very high tension and can cause severe injuries if a winding bar slips or a spring releases unexpectedly. Multiple garage door repair sources specifically caution that torsion spring replacement is not a typical DIY job. [How Much Does It Actually Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring?](https://www.thespruce.com/garage-door-spring-replacement-8635322?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

A reasonable DIY project for an experienced homeowner might be replacing some **extension springs** (the springs that run alongside the horizontal tracks), but even then there is risk and careful safety procedures are required. For torsion springs, the risk-to-savings ratio is usually not favorable. [How Much Does It Actually Cost to Replace a Garage Door Spring?](https://www.thespruce.com/garage-door-spring-replacement-8635322?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

Regarding hiring Valiant Garage Door:

  * I found a few review sources showing generally positive feedback for a company named Valiant Garage Door, including positive ratings on BBB, Angi, and HomeAdvisor. [Valiant Garage Door | BBB Business Profile | Better Business Bureau](https://www.bbb.org/us/ca/san-francisco/profile/garage-doors/valiant-garage-door-llc-1116-980936?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Valiant Garage Door Reviews - Pleasanton, CA | Angi](https://www.angi.com/companylist/us/ca/pleasanton/valiant-garage-door-llc-reviews-1.htm?utm_source=chatgpt.com)[Valiant Garage Door Reviews - Hollister, CA | HomeAdvisor](https://www.homeadvisor.com/rated.valiantgaragedoor.100095056.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * I also found some complaints and mixed experiences, which is normal for many service businesses. [Valiant Garage Doors Reviews, Complaints, Customer Service](https://www.revdex.com/reviews/valiant-garage-doors-llc/2068049?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

  * Based on the information available, I don't see a clear red flag that would make me advise against getting a quote from them, but I would still recommend getting **2–3 quotes** for spring replacement. Garage door spring pricing can vary substantially, and homeowners frequently report being offered additional parts and upgrades that may or may not be necessary. [spring replacement/upsell question](https://www.reddit.com/r/GarageDoorService/comments/1ua4pc2/spring_replacementupsell_question/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

One thing to watch for: if a technician recommends replacing cables, drums, bearings, or other hardware along with the springs, ask them to show you the wear and explain why each part needs replacement. Sometimes it's justified; sometimes it's optional maintenance. [spring replacement/upsell question](https://www.reddit.com/r/GarageDoorService/comments/1ua4pc2/spring_replacementupsell_question/?utm_source=chatgpt.com)

If you tell me:

  1. Whether the spring is a **torsion** or **extension** spring,

  2. Whether it's broken already or you're replacing it proactively,

  3. Your approximate location,

I can give a more specific recommendation and a typical price range for your area.
